symptom: ARM API returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests when subscription or tenant exceeds throttling limits
rootCause: ARM enforces sliding 1-hour window limits: 1200 writes and 32000 reads per subscription/tenant. Automated scripts or many concurrent users can exhaust these limits.
solution: Reduce polling frequency of automated scripts; scope queries to specific resources instead of scanning all; distribute workload across subscriptions. Use x-ms-ratelimit-remaining-* response headers to monitor remaining quota. Query HttpIncomingRequests in ARM Kusto to analyze request patterns by clientIpAddress and clientApplicationId.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======14. PORTAL & ARM=======/14.6 [ARM]ARM Throttling.md

symptom: 403 Forbidden when creating Azure subscriptions via billing API (Microsoft.Subscription/aliases) using managed identity with Contributor permission on billing account
rootCause: The workload parameter in the Create Subscription Aliases request must match the enabledAzurePlans of the invoice section. Customer used workload=DevTest but the invoice section only had skuId=0001 (Microsoft Azure Plan/Production) enabled.
solution: Check the enabledAzurePlans of the invoice section via ListInvoiceSectionsWithCreateSubscriptionPermission API. Use workload=Production instead of DevTest if the billing account only has the Production Azure Plan (skuId 0001) enabled. The workload must comply with the billing account enabled plans.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======24. Billing API (21v Customer Agreement)====/24.1 Received 403 forbidden when using billing API.md

symptom: ARM template deployment of Azure SQL DB elasticpools fails intermittently with InternalServerError during Azure DevOps pipeline deployment
rootCause: Concurrent ARM operations on the same SQL Server cause resource contention. The ARM template triggers multiple dependent operations (elasticpools + server config changes) simultaneously after SQL Server creation, leading to Server is busy with another operation error.
solution: Add dependsOn in ARM template to ensure sequential execution: elasticpools and other SQL Server config changes should depend on both the SQL Server and each other. Use ARM Kusto (armmcadx.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/armmc HttpIncomingRequests) to find serviceRequestId, then query SQL Kusto (sqlazurechi2.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/MoonCake MonManagement/MonManagementOperations) for detailed error.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======DB=======/[MCVKB]如何排查使用ARM部署Azure SQL DB elasticpools  资源失败 (2).md

symptom: ARM template deployment fails with request content size exceeds the maximum size of 4MB
rootCause: ARM enforces a hard 4MB limit on template file size that cannot be changed.
solution: Split large template into multiple smaller templates and deploy via Linked Templates (templateLink.uri must be HTTP/HTTPS accessible, e.g., Storage Account). Alternatively, use Template Spec: upload linked templates first, then reference them by resource ID in the main template. Template Spec is free and requires Template Spec Contributor RBAC role.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Dev=======/5.1 ARM部署模板_4MB的替代方案与Linked Template方案中存储账号开启防火墙后无.md

symptom: Linked Template deployment returns 403 SASIpAuthorization error when Storage Account has firewall enabled, even after adding ARM Service Tag IPv4 addresses to whitelist
rootCause: ARM Service Tags contain both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses. Storage Account firewall only supports IPv4 whitelist configuration. When ARM service instance uses IPv6 address to access the linked template, the request is blocked by the firewall.
solution: Use Template Spec instead of Linked Templates stored in firewall-protected Storage Account. Microsoft documentation explicitly states Linked Template is not compatible with Storage Account firewall. Upload linked templates to Template Spec, then reference by resource ID in main template.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Dev=======/5.1 ARM部署模板_4MB的替代方案与Linked Template方案中存储账号开启防火墙后无.md

symptom: Azure Automation runbook using Set-AzAutomationModule cmdlet fails to update Az modules; runbook times out after 3 hours with no error in job stream
rootCause: Known issue in Azure PowerShell cmdlet (GitHub #16399): Set-AzAutomationModule cannot upload PowerShell 7.1 modules. Additionally, the open-source update script enters deadlock loop when dependent Az sub-modules are missing.
solution: Use REST API to replicate portal operations: capture exact API calls via F12 browser dev tools on Azure Portal, extract method/URL/payload from batch API call (management.chinacloudapi.cn/batch), then implement in runbook using Invoke-RestMethod with MSI authentication. PATCH the automationAccount with RuntimeConfiguration to update modules.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======16. Automation======/16.7 How to use REST API to do azure portal operat.md

symptom: ARM template deployment of Application Gateway with Key Vault SSL certificates fails with identity value error when using exported template
rootCause: Exported ARM template includes principalId and clientId in the managed identity section under Resources. These auto-generated values are specific to the original deployment and cause deployment failure when reused.
solution: Edit the exported ARM template: find the identity section under Resources, delete the principalId and clientId fields. Keep only the type and userAssignedIdentities reference. Save and redeploy. Applies to both Azure China and Global Azure.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/1.5[NETVKB]How to deploy APPGW by template if SSL.md

symptom: AKS cluster scaling (e.g. 6 to 35 nodes) fails with cse-agent extension timeout, resulting in missing nodes and cluster unhealthy state
rootCause: During AKS node scale-out, the ARM->CRP->CSE pipeline can timeout when provisioning large numbers of nodes simultaneously. The cse-agent CustomScriptExtension responsible for node bootstrapping fails to complete within the allotted time.
solution: Troubleshoot via multi-layer Kusto: 1) AKS Kusto (akscn.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/AKSprod) for cluster-level operations; 2) ARM Kusto (armmcadx.chinaeast2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/armmc HttpIncomingRequests) for deployment flow; 3) CRP Kusto for VM extension details. Check CSE logs on failed node. For large scale operations, consider scaling in smaller batches.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Case Study###/[AKS][ARM][ARM-_ CRP] AKS cluster scaling failed w.md

symptom: AKS node scale-out takes 30+ minutes to complete instead of expected 5-10 minutes
rootCause: During VMSS scale-out, CRP calls NRP to update associated resources. If AKS cluster has an Application Gateway v1 (CloudService type) associated, the AppGW update operation alone can take 15-30+ minutes, dominating the total scale-out duration.
solution: Troubleshoot via multi-layer Kusto tracing: 1) ARM HttpIncomingRequests (filter PUT/PATCH on VMSS by subscriptionId+timeframe) to get correlationId; 2) CRP ApiQosEvent (filter by correlationId) to get operationId; 3) CRP ContextActivity (filter by activityId=operationId) to find slow downstream calls to NRP; 4) NRP Jarvis logs to confirm AppGW operation duration. Root fix: migrate from AppGW v1 to v2 which has faster update operations.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Case Study###/[AKS][ARM_CRP_NRP] AKS node scale out take 30+ min.md

symptom: AVD/WVD host pool VM deployment fails in a subnet that has PaaS service (e.g. App Service Environment/Hosting Environment) association
rootCause: Azure does not support creating IaaS resources (VMs) in a subnet that has PaaS service integration (e.g. Microsoft.Web/hostingEnvironments). The subnet delegation/service association blocks VM deployment.
solution: Either 1) Remove the external service association link (e.g. hostingEnvironments) from the target subnet, then redeploy AVD host pool VMs; or 2) Deploy AVD VMs to a different subnet without PaaS integration. If removing the association does not resolve the issue, engage the hosting environments (Microsoft.Web) team.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Global Case Study/Week 2 - 1st Nov 2021/[Wei] 2106110060000413 _ Issue deploying WVD host.md

symptom: Customer needs to grant full database access (DB owner level) across multiple database types (SQL, DBForMySQL) in a single resource group to one user
rootCause: Built-in RBAC roles only cover specific database types individually (e.g. SQL DB Contributor). There is no built-in role that covers multiple DB types like SQL + DBForMySQL together.
solution: Create a custom RBAC role combining multiple DB type permissions: use Microsoft.Sql/* and Microsoft.DBforMySQL/* in actions array. Also include supporting permissions from built-in roles: Microsoft.Authorization/*/read, Microsoft.Insights/alertRules/*, Microsoft.ResourceHealth/availabilityStatuses/read, Microsoft.Resources/deployments/*, Microsoft.Insights/metrics/read. Set assignableScopes at subscription level for reusability. Assign the custom role at resource group scope.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure AD _ Ms Entra ID/Account management/RBAC/Case study/Custom RBAC role for multiple DB access in resourc.md

symptom: Azure Arc server custom script extension provisioning failed (CSE provisioning error)
rootCause: Custom script extension (CustomScriptExtension) deployment on Arc-enabled servers can fail due to misconfigured extension settings or connectivity issues between the Arc agent and the extension service.
solution: 1) Verify Arc agent connectivity and status via azcmagent show; 2) Check extension logs on the machine; 3) Ensure correct publisher (Microsoft.Azure.Extensions for Linux, Microsoft.Compute for Windows) and extension type; 4) Use Set-AzConnectedMachineExtension with correct -Publisher, -ExtensionType, -Settings parameters.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Arc Enabled Servers=====/#Case records and study..md

symptom: Azure Arc onboarding script fails to add on-premises server when Azure Arc Private Link is configured
rootCause: When Azure Arc Private Link Scope is configured, the onboarding script requires proper DNS resolution and network connectivity through the private endpoint. Misconfigured DNS or missing private endpoints for required Arc services block the onboarding process.
solution: 1) Verify all required private endpoints are created for Azure Arc Private Link Scope (Microsoft.HybridCompute, Microsoft.GuestConfiguration); 2) Ensure DNS resolution for Arc service endpoints resolves to private IP addresses; 3) Check proxy/firewall rules allow traffic to private endpoints; 4) Validate private DNS zone configuration (privatelink.his.arc.azure.cn for Mooncake).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Arc Enabled Servers=====/#Case records and study..md

symptom: Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) install fails on Arc-enabled server in China North 3 region with HCRP500 error: 'Unexpected error occurred while processing the request'
rootCause: Region-specific issue in China North 3 affecting AMA extension installation on Arc-enabled servers. Tracked by ICM-461450990.
solution: Workaround: Deploy the Arc-enabled server in a different region (e.g., China North 2, China East 2) instead of China North 3. Monitor ICM-461450990 for permanent fix.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Arc Enabled Servers=====/1.On going issue for Arc server(30th Jan.).md

symptom: Arc VM extension stuck in 'updating' state indefinitely when updating the same extension with identical or new settings repeatedly
rootCause: Updating the same Arc connected machine extension repeatedly (via Update-AzConnectedMachineExtension) without changes or too quickly causes the extension to enter a permanent 'updating' state that never completes, eventually timing out with no actual operation performed.
solution: 1) Avoid updating the same extension repeatedly without necessity; 2) If stuck, remove the extension (Remove-AzConnectedMachineExtension) and re-create it (Set-AzConnectedMachineExtension); 3) When updating, always change at least one setting to ensure the update is meaningful; 4) Use Get-AzConnectedMachineExtension to verify current state before issuing updates.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Arc Enabled Servers=====/2.How to deploy a customscriprtextension using pow.md

symptom: Bicep deployment fails or targets wrong cloud environment (deploying to AzureCloud instead of AzureChinaCloud/Mooncake)
rootCause: Bicep defaults to AzureCloud profile. Without explicit bicepconfig.json setting currentProfile to AzureChinaCloud, Bicep module registry operations and deployments will target the global Azure endpoints instead of Mooncake endpoints.
solution: Create bicepconfig.json in the Bicep files directory with: {"cloud": {"currentProfile": "AzureChinaCloud", "profiles": {"AzureChinaCloud": {"resourceManagerEndpoint": "https://management.chinacloudapi.cn", "activeDirectoryAuthority": "https://login.chinacloudapi.cn"}}, "credentialPrecedence": ["AzureCLI", "AzurePowerShell"]}}. Ensure az cloud set --name AzureChinaCloud or Connect-AzAccount -Environment AzureChinaCloud is done before deployment.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/DEPLOYMENT/## Bicep/Config Env to Mooncake.md

symptom: Customer asks about Azure Arc-enabled SQL Server availability in Mooncake (Azure China)
rootCause: Arc-enabled SQL Server is not available in Mooncake. The service has not been deployed to Azure China sovereign cloud.
solution: Inform customer Arc-enabled SQL Server is not supported in Mooncake. For Arc-enabled server collab (non-SQL) refer to ADO wiki AzureArcforSQLServer.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Arc Enabled Servers=====/#Support boundaries_scope..md

symptom: Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) installed on Arc-enabled server but no data appears in Log Analytics workspace
rootCause: AMA requires a Data Collection Rule (DCR) to be associated. Without DCR, AMA is installed but does not collect or forward any data.
solution: Create DCR and associate with Arc-enabled server. Deploy AMA via Portal/PowerShell/Azure Policy. Use AMA troubleshooter (Windows) to diagnose. For live troubleshooting follow MS Learn guide for AMA on Windows Arc-enabled server.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Arc Enabled Servers=====/#VM Extensions/AzureMonitorAgent(AMA).md

symptom: Azure Monitor extension deploy fails on Arc-enabled K8s cluster in Mooncake. S500 customer (Siemens) reports extension creation failures across multiple cases.
rootCause: Arc-enabled K8s extension deployment issues in Mooncake, potentially related to endpoint availability or extension compatibility in sovereign cloud.
solution: Check extension compatibility for Mooncake. Verify Arc K8s agent connectivity. Confirm agent version supports Mooncake endpoints. Escalate to Arc K8s PG if needed. Ref cases: 2310170040001714, 2308030040000993.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Azure Arc for Kubernetes====/#Cusotmer_siemens.md

symptom: AkvSecretProviderExtension v1.4.2 fails to install on Arc-enabled K8s cluster in Mooncake
rootCause: Extension version compatibility or endpoint issue in Mooncake sovereign cloud. Part of broader Arc K8s extension deployment problems.
solution: Verify AkvSecretProviderExtension version availability in Mooncake. Check if newer version resolves issue. Confirm cluster connectivity and agent status. Case: 2311280040002131.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Azure Arc for Kubernetes====/#Cusotmer_siemens.md

symptom: Azure Policy (Microsoft.PolicyInsights) and Microsoft Defender for Containers (microsoft.azuredefender.kubernetes) extensions are not available on Arc-enabled K8s clusters in Mooncake. Portal extension install page is empty; extensions can only be installed via CLI.
rootCause: These extensions have not been onboarded to the Mooncake (Azure China) environment. The portal UI for Arc K8s extension installation is not functional in Mooncake.
solution: Use Azure CLI to install available extensions (azuremonitor.containers, azurekeyvaultsecretsprovider, microsoft.flux). For Azure Policy and Defender, inform customer these are not yet available in Mooncake and there is no published ETA.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Azure Arc for Kubernetes====/#Extension status.md

symptom: Kusto telemetry is not available for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes in Mooncake. Engineer cannot query Arc K8s diagnostic data via Kusto clusters.
rootCause: Arc-enabled K8s Kusto integration has not been deployed to the Mooncake environment.
solution: Use Jarvis (Geneva monitoring portal) as the alternative diagnostic tool. Jarvis link: https://portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/3CCA1DE0. Refer to ADO Wiki for Kusto connection guidance once available: https://msazure.visualstudio.com/One/_wiki/wikis/One.wiki/98547/Connect-to-Kusto
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Arc/====Azure Arc for Kubernetes====/#kusto&jarvis.md

symptom: VMware agentless migration initial replication stuck at 'queued' status with 0% progress. ProgressPercentage stays at 0, UploadedBytes=0 while TotalBytesToTransfer shows full disk size.
rootCause: Snapshot replication cycle initiated but data transfer not progressing. Common causes: Gateway worker scheduling delays (RequestTime/ScheduledTime gaps), vCenter connectivity issues, or Storage Account access problems for the cache/log storage in Mooncake.
solution: 1) Query SRSOperationEvent in ASR Kusto (asradxclusmc.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/ASRKustoDB) filtering by SubscriptionId to check operation states. 2) Query SRSDataEvent with ClientRequestId filtering Message for 'InitialReplication' to track IR progress. 3) Query GatewayOperationEvent for SnapshotReplication events to check upload statistics. 4) Query GatewayDiagnosticEvent for worker scheduling (RequestTime vs ScheduledTime) to identify scheduling delays. 5) Run appliance diagnostics at https://localhost:44368.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Migrate  (Deprecated)/## Case Study/[migrate] VMware Agentless _ initial replication q.md

symptom: Multiple VMware/Physical VMs with same BIOS UUID/GUID cause Azure Migrate discovery to show duplicate machines or fail validation
rootCause: When VMs are cloned without regenerating BIOS UUID, multiple machines report identical BIOSGuid to Azure Migrate, causing deduplication issues in the discovery service
solution: Use Kusto query against AMPKustoDB.Messages to identify machines sharing same BIOSGuid. Fix by regenerating BIOS UUID on cloned VMs (vm.changeableByDesign.uuid in VMware settings) or using Physical server discovery method which validates by IP+BIOS combination
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Migrate  (Deprecated)/## Troubleshooting/Kusto Query/Discovery (VM).md

symptom: Azure Migrate SQL discovery fails with 'Failed to fetch details for sql server' error for one or more SQL Server instances
rootCause: SQL discovery agent cannot access SQL Server metadata due to credential issues, network problems, or SQL Server configuration. The AgentException in Kusto logs provides specific failure reason
solution: 1) Identify failing SQL servers via Kusto query filtering AMPKustoDB.Messages for 'Failed to fetch details for sql server' with the SQL agent ID. 2) Check RunAsAccount credentials match in appliance config. 3) Verify SQL Server allows remote connections. 4) Use ServiceActivityId for detailed error trace
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Migrate  (Deprecated)/## Troubleshooting/Kusto Query/Discovery (SQL).md

symptom: Azure Migrate dependency map guest data collection fails with errors like GuestCredentialNotValid, VMWareToolStatusIsNotInstalled, ProcessExitedWithNonZeroExitCode, or WindowsUACIntervention
rootCause: Customer-side errors (CxErrors): invalid guest credentials, VM powered off, VMware Tools not installed/running, insufficient privileges. Non-customer errors (NonCxErrors): VimException, script execution timeout, temporary user profile, null result
solution: Use Kusto query against AMPKustoDB.Messages filtering 'Dependency Map guest data collection completed' with agent ID to classify errors. For CxErrors: fix credentials, install VMware Tools, grant privileges. For NonCxErrors: check appliance connectivity, retry, or escalate to PG with ICM
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Migrate  (Deprecated)/## Troubleshooting/Kusto Query/Discovery (Apps and Dependency).md

symptom: Azure Migrate fails to collect SQL Server performance data with 'Failed to get performance data for Sql server' error, causing incomplete assessment
rootCause: SQL agent cannot collect performance counters from SQL Server instance. Common causes: insufficient permissions on sys.dm_os_performance_counters, SQL Server service not running, network timeout between appliance and SQL Server
solution: 1) Query AMPKustoDB.Messages with SQL agent ID filtering 'Failed to get performance data for Sql server' to identify failing instances. 2) Verify RunAsAccount has VIEW SERVER STATE permission. 3) Check SQL Server is accessible from appliance. 4) Use ServiceActivityId to drill into detailed error logs
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Migrate  (Deprecated)/## Troubleshooting/Kusto Query/Discovery (SQL).md

symptom: Need to escalate Azure Migrate issue to product group (PG) via ICM
rootCause: Complex Azure Migrate issues that require PG investigation cannot be resolved at CSS level
solution: 1) Create ICM using template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=r2V1r1. 2) Post message on Azure Migrate Teams Channel (PG HELP). 3) Include appliance machine ID, subscription ID, agent IDs, and relevant Kusto query results in the ICM
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Migrate  (Deprecated)/## Troubleshooting/ICM.md

symptom: Resource move (NIC between resource groups) fails with HTTP 409 ResourceMoveProviderValidationFailed / MoveCannotProceedWithResourcesNotInSucceededState / AnotherOperationInProgress when concurrent CRUD operations (e.g., Databricks VM deletion) are active on resources in the same VNet
rootCause: ARM resource move validation checks ALL resources in the same VNet. If any resource has an ongoing operation (not in Succeeded state), the entire move is blocked. In this case, Databricks VM tenant deletion operations were running concurrently, blocking NIC move.
solution: 1) Use Kusto FrontendOperationEtwEvent to find the blocker operationId from the 409 error correlationId. 2) Query the blocker operation to identify what resource/operation is blocking. 3) Wait for the concurrent operation to complete, then retry the move. 4) For network resources, ensure no CDUR activities are in progress on any resource in the same VNet before initiating move.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Resource.md

symptom: Resource group location property shows CN1/CE1 even though all resources are in CN3/CE3 after migration. Cannot change the location property of an existing resource group through Azure portal or ARM API.
rootCause: ARM does not support changing the location property of a resource group after creation. This is a platform limitation — the RG location is metadata-only and does not affect resource placement, but causes confusion during CN1/CE1 region retirement.
solution: 1) Use PG-provided PowerShell scripts (project-scrub-jay) to change RG location via Resource Mover — contact frances.hu@microsoft.com for access. 2) PG is developing a backend solution to rehome RG location for both 1P and 3P users (timeline TBD as of Mar 2026). 3) Note: VNET-integrated/injected resources may not support Resource Mover. Always verify in customer environment before executing.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Resource.md

symptom: After moving a Log Analytics Workspace to a new resource group, linked services (Diagnostic Settings, AKS Insights, Data Collection Rules) report 'resource not found' errors and may prevent AKS nodes from starting
rootCause: Moving a LAW to a new resource group changes its ARM Resource ID. All dependent services referencing the old Resource ID (Diagnostic Settings, AKS Container Insights, DCR associations) break because they still point to the old path.
solution: 1) Stop or disable all dependent services BEFORE the move: Diagnostic Settings, AKS Insights, DCR associations. 2) Perform the resource group move. 3) Reconfigure all dependent services with the new LAW Resource ID after the move. 4) For AKS, verify nodes can start successfully after reconfiguration.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Resource.md

symptom: MySQL Flexible Server with VNet injection: cannot move the VNet of a VNet-injected server to another resource group via Resource Mover. Moving a server with read replica also fails.
rootCause: ARM Resource Mover does not support moving VNets that have PaaS resources (MySQL flex) deployed into them. Replica dependency also blocks cross-RG move.
solution: For VNet-injected MySQL: only move the server itself (works, no downtime). Do not attempt to move the associated VNet. For servers with read replica: remove the replica before moving, then recreate after move.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Resource.md

symptom: Azure Data Explorer (ADX/Kusto) cluster with Private Endpoint cannot be moved to another resource group. Moving single ADX with PE fails. Moving ADX + PE together also fails validation.
rootCause: ARM resource move validation does not support moving ADX clusters that have associated Private Endpoints. The validation step rejects the combined move.
solution: Move single ADX cluster without PE (works with no significant interruption, only some query delay observed). For ADX with PE: delete the PE, move the ADX cluster, then recreate the PE in the target resource group.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Resource.md

symptom: App Service/Function App restarts after resource group move when Application Insights is configured. Business interruption occurs during the move.
rootCause: Moving an App Service with Application Insights configured triggers a restart of the App Service/Function App as part of the move operation.
solution: Plan for brief downtime during move. Check all dependencies before moving: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/move-support-resources#microsoftweb. Consider disabling App Insights before move and re-enabling after.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Resource.md

symptom: Application Gateway cannot be moved between resource groups via ARM Resource Mover. AppGW WAF Policy also does not support move.
rootCause: Application Gateway does not have native resource move support in ARM. No API for backup/restore of configuration.
solution: Export the existing AppGW configuration as ARM template, deploy the same configuration in the new resource group. Note: dependencies (VNET, subnet, public IP, SSL certs, WAF policy) must be handled separately. Reference case: 2511110040001280.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Resource.md

symptom: Azure Storage cross-region live migration gets stuck or aborts when storage account has sustained ingress bandwidth > 1 Gbps during migration. Migration appears stuck with no available incremental resume.
rootCause: Live cross-region migration is a single atomic operation using geo-replication machinery. High sustained writes prevent the migration from converging. CRC/checksum errors during transfer cause full abort and restart. No concept of resume-from-last-blob.
solution: Throttle or freeze writes during migration. Prefer ADF/AzCopy for Mooncake: create new storage account in target region, copy data with incremental sync, then cutover. Live migration only if account identity must be preserved, account is LRS, and sustained ingress < 1 Gbps.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - PG Storage Migration Guid.md

symptom: After Azure SQL geo-replication failover (promoting secondary), application cannot authenticate — logins return access denied or orphaned user errors on the new primary.
rootCause: Geo-replication copies database-level Users automatically but does NOT copy server-level Logins. If Logins are not manually recreated with matching SIDs on the target server, users become orphaned after failover.
solution: Before failover: export all logins with SIDs from source (sys.sql_logins), create matching logins on target server with same SID using CREATE LOGIN ... WITH SID = <hex>. Verify SID match on target via sys.database_principals JOIN sys.sql_logins. For Entra ID auth, ensure same Entra admin on target.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - SQL Migration Guide.md

symptom: ContainerApps with VNET Integration cannot use Resource Mover for cross-region migration in Azure China.
rootCause: ContainerApps with VNET Integration do not support Resource Mover. Azure China does not support Resource Mover for ContainerApps without VNET Integration either (Global Azure does support it).
solution: Only option is to rebuild ContainerApps in the new region. For customers needing Resource Mover support for non-VNET ContainerApps in Mooncake, request feature parity from PG. Contact Eng Leader: bowan@microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/[Team]Process and IcM and Common Tools/Project-based Support/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 (Mooncake CSS only)/CN1_CE1 Migration 防坑指南 - Migration Plan - Service.md
